title: 3005: Holy Spirit, qualities of
content: • 3010: God, the Holy Spirit

title: 3010: God, the Holy Spirit
content: The co-equal and co-eternal Spirit of the Father and the Son, who inspired Scripture and brings new life to the people of God. The Spirit of God is often portrayed in Scripture in terms of “breath”, “life” or “wind”, indicating his role in sustaining and bringing life to God's creation.

title: 3015: Holy Spirit, divinity of
content:
The Holy Spirit's attributes and activities are always those of God. The titles used of the Holy Spirit identify him as part of the triune nature of the divine being.

The Holy Spirit possesses the attributes of God

He is present everywhere

PSA 139:7–139:8 See also JHN 14:17

He knows all things

ISA 40:13 NIV footnote. See also JHN 16:13; 1CO 2:10–2:11

He has infinite power

ZEC 4:6 See also LUK 1:35

He is eternal

HEB 9:14 See also JHN 14:16

He is unique

EPH 4:4–4:6 See also 1CO 12:4

He is holy

ROM 1:4 See also ROM 5:5; 1CO 6:19 The Christian's body is also holy because it is the place where God dwells by his Spirit; 2CO 6:6EPH 1:13, EPH 4:30 1TH 1:5; 2TI 1:14; TIT 3:5; HEB 2:4, HEB 9:8; 1PE 1:12; 2PE 1:21; JUD 1:20

The Holy Spirit performs divine works

He creates

PSA 104:30 See also GEN 1:2; JOB 26:13, JOB 33:4; PSA 33:6

He gives life

EZK 37:14 See also GEN 2:7; ROM 8:2, ROM 8:11

The Holy Spirit is identified with the person and activity of the Godhead

Holy Spirit'' andGod'' are used interchangeably

ACT 5:3–5:4 See also 1CO 3:16

The Spirit is called ``Lord''

2CO 3:17–3:18

It is possible to blaspheme the Spirit

See also MAT 12:32; MRK 3:29; LUK 12:10

The Spirit is sent by the Father and the Son

JHN 15:26 See also JHN 16:14–16:15

The Spirit is identified with the Father and the Son

MAT 28:19–28:20 in commissioning; ; 2CO 13:14 in benediction; ; EPH 2:18 in access to God; GAL 4:6; ; 1PE 1:1–1:2

The Holy Spirit is given divine titles

The Spirit of God

2CH 15:1 See also EXO 31:3; NUM 24:2; 1SA 10:10 Saul prophesies at Gibeah; 1SA 19:20; 2CH 24:20; EZK 11:24; MAT 3:16 at Jesus Christ's baptism; 1CO 2:11, 1CO 2:14; 2CO 3:3; PHP 3:3; 1PE 4:14

The Spirit of the LORD

JDG 3:10; ISA 61:1 See also JDG 6:34; 1SA 16:13–16:14; 2SA 23:2; 1KI 22:24; 2KI 2:16; 2CH 18:23; ISA 11:2, ISA 63:14; EZK 11:5, EZK 37:1

The Spirit of the Lord

LUK 4:18; ; 2CO 3:17

The Spirit of Christ

ROM 8:9 See also GAL 4:6; PHP 1:19; 1PE 1:11

The Spirit of Jesus

ACT 16:7

resource.label: 3110: Holy Spirit, titles and names of

title: 3020: Holy Spirit, joy of
content:
The Holy Spirit brings joy to believers, giving them an inner contentment and happiness which is not dependent upon external circumstances.

The Holy Spirit enables the Messiah to bring joy

ISA 61:1–61:3 See also ISA 9:2–9:3

The Holy Spirit gives joy

To those anticipating Jesus Christ's birth

LUK 1:41–1:45 See also LUK 1:67–1:68

To Jesus Christ

LUK 10:21 See also HEB 1:9

To disciples even in the face of opposition

ACT 13:52 See also ACT 16:25; 1TH 1:6; 1PE 4:13–4:14

The Holy Spirit's fruit includes joy

GAL 5:22 See also ROM 14:17, ROM 15:13

Evidence of the Holy Spirit's work prompts believers to rejoice

ACT 11:15–11:18 See also ACT 11:23–11:24

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 3025: Holy Spirit, personality of
content:
The Holy Spirit is not an impersonal power but a real person with his own personality.

The Holy Spirit is involved in personal relationships

He is in relationship with the Father and the Son

MAT 28:19 See also JHN 16:14–16:15; 2CO 13:14; 1PE 1:2; JUD 1:20–1:21

He is in relationship with Christians

ACT 15:28

The Spirit can be treated in personal ways

ACT 5:3 See also ACT 5:9; HEB 10:29

Personal characteristics of the Holy Spirit

The Spirit is wise

ISA 11:2; EPH 1:17 See also JHN 14:26; ROM 8:27

The Spirit can experience emotion and pain

EPH 4:30 See also ISA 63:10; HEB 10:29

Grammatical indications of the Holy Spirit's personhood

JHN 14:16 See also JHN 14:26, JHN 15:26, JHN 16:7

Examples of the Holy Spirit's actions

He teaches: LUK 12:12; ; 1CO 2:13; ROM 8:16 He testifies; ; ROM 8:26 He intercedes; ; ACT 20:23 He warns; 2SA 23:2; ; ACT 8:29, ACT 10:19, ACT 11:12, ACT 13:2; ; REV 2:7; 1CO 2:11 He knows; ; GAL 4:6 He calls out; ; JHN 16:13 He hears

The Holy Spirit's own personality is seen by the gifts he gives and the attitudes he promotes

GAL 5:22–5:23 See also LUK 10:21; ACT 9:31; ROM 5:5, ROM 8:6, ROM 14:17, ROM 15:13; 2CO 13:14; 1JN 1:6, 1JN 5:6

resource.label: 3110: Holy Spirit, titles and names of

title: 3030: Holy Spirit, power of
content:
The Holy Spirit equips and empowers believers so that the reign and reality of God is revealed through them in the world.

The power of the Holy Spirit is witnessed to in the OT

ISA 11:2–11:3 See also ISA 42:1

The Spirit will show his power on the earth

ISA 32:15

The Spirit's power will enable people to serve God

EZK 36:26–36:27 See also JER 31:33

The Spirit's power will prompt prophecy and visions

JOL 2:28–2:29 See also ACT 2:17–2:18

The Holy Spirit's power is described by OT imagery

Oil: 1SA 10:1, 1SA 16:13; ISA 63:11–63:12 the arm of God; EZK 3:14, EZK 37:1

The Holy Spirit's power is demonstrated in the OT

In creation

GEN 1:2; ; JOB 33:4; ; PSA 104:30

In acts of judgment and war

JDG 14:19 See also JDG 3:10, JDG 6:34, JDG 11:29, JDG 14:6, JDG 15:14; 1SA 11:6, 1SA 16:13

In the lives of his servants

MIC 3:8 See also NUM 11:17; 1SA 10:6, 1SA 10:10

The Holy Spirit's power is seen in the life of Jesus Christ

In his conception

LUK 1:35

In his teaching and ministry

See also MAT 7:28–7:29, MAT 12:28; MRK 1:22, MRK 1:27; LUK 4:14, LUK 5:17; ACT 10:38

In his resurrection

ROM 1:4 See also ROM 8:11; 1TI 3:16; 1PE 3:18

The Holy Spirit's power is seen in the church's mission

In the church's witness and preaching

ACT 1:8 See also LUK 24:49; ACT 6:10, ACT 16:7; 1CO 2:4; 1TH 1:5

In the apostolic ministry of signs and wonders

ROM 15:18–15:19 See also HEB 2:4

In miraculous works in the church

GAL 3:5 See also 1CO 12:28

The Holy Spirit's power builds Christian character

ROM 15:13 See also 2TI 1:7

The Holy Spirit's power strengthens the church

EPH 3:16 See also ROM 1:11; 1CO 1:7–1:8

title: 3035: Holy Spirit, presence of
content:
Present throughout the world, the Holy Spirit convicts the world of sin and brings peace and comfort to believers through his refreshing and restoring presence.

The Holy Spirit is present and active in creation

GEN 1:2 See also JOB 33:4; PSA 104:30

The Holy Spirit is present everywhere

PSA 139:7 See also ACT 20:23

He is equated with God's presence

PSA 51:11

He is present with God's people

HAG 2:5 See also ISA 59:21, ISA 63:10–63:14; EZK 36:27, EZK 37:14; 1CO 3:16; EPH 2:22

He is present in Jesus Christ's ministry

LUK 4:14, LUK 4:18, LUK 5:17, LUK 6:19, LUK 10:21; ; ACT 10:38

He is present with Jesus Christ's disciples

JHN 14:16–14:17 See also JHN 14:26, JHN 15:26–15:27

The consequences of the Holy Spirit's presence in the church

He convicts of sin

JHN 16:7–16:11; ; 1TH 1:5

He gives direction

ACT 13:2, ACT 15:28

He transforms experience

PHP 1:19

He encourages

ACT 9:31

He empowers Christians for witness

See also ACT 1:8, ACT 6:10

He sanctifies

2CO 3:18 See also ROM 15:16; 2TH 2:13; 1PE 1:2

The Holy Spirit's presence has observable effects

2CO 3:3 See also JHN 3:21; ACT 11:23

He brings blessing and fellowship

2CO 13:14 See also PHP 2:1; 1PE 4:14

He brings freedom and peace

2CO 3:17 See also ROM 8:2, ROM 8:5–8:9

He brings life

ROM 8:10–8:11

title: 3040: Holy Spirit, promise of
content:
The OT foretold and Jesus Christ reaffirmed that the Holy Spirit would be poured out on God's people, transforming, empowering and gifting them.

OT promises of the Holy Spirit fulfilled in the OT

The promised Spirit enables prophecy

1SA 10:6 See also 1SA 10:10

The Spirit fulfils the promised presence of the LORD

EXO 33:14 See also ISA 63:11, ISA 63:14; HAG 2:5

OT promises of the Holy Spirit fulfilled in the NT

The Spirit implements the promised new covenant

JER 31:33–31:34 See also ROM 8:2, ROM 8:9–8:10; 2CO 3:3, 2CO 3:6; HEB 8:10–8:11

The promised Spirit will renew Israel

EZK 36:26–36:27 See also EZK 11:19, EZK 37:14

The promised Spirit is the guarantee for Israel's future

ISA 44:3 See also ISA 32:15, ISA 59:21; EZK 39:29

The promised Spirit will empower the life and work of the Messiah

ISA 61:1–61:2 See also ISA 11:2; LUK 4:18–4:19

The Spirit is promised for all people

JOL 2:28–2:29 See also ACT 2:17–2:18

Promises of the Holy Spirit in the NT

The promise that Jesus Christ would baptise with the Spirit

MAT 3:11 parallels MRK 1:8, LUK 3:16 See also JHN 1:33; ACT 1:5, ACT 11:16

The Spirit is promised by Jesus Christ

LUK 24:49 See also JHN 15:26, JHN 16:7–16:15; ACT 1:5–1:8

The Spirit is promised for the time after Jesus Christ's resurrection and ascension

JHN 7:39 See also ACT 2:4, ACT 2:33

The promised Spirit fulfils God's word to Abraham

GAL 3:14

The promised Spirit guarantees to believers their future inheritance

EPH 1:13–1:14

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 3045: Holy Spirit, sovereignty of
content:
The Holy Spirit has supreme power and acts in accordance with divine purposes, exercising authority and control in the world and over God's people.

The Holy Spirit is Lord

2CO 3:17–3:18 See also JDG 3:10

The Holy Spirit is sovereign in bringing spiritual life

JHN 3:1–3:8 See also EZK 37:14; JHN 6:63; ROM 8:2; TIT 3:5; 1PE 1:2

The Holy Spirit's will is sovereign

He determines the distribution of spiritual gifts

1CO 12:11 See also 1SA 10:6, 1SA 10:10, 1SA 19:20, 1SA 19:23; HEB 2:4

He controls the appointment of leaders

ACT 13:2 See also ACT 20:28

The Holy Spirit demonstrates his sovereignty over creation

He participates in the work of creation

JOB 33:4 See also GEN 1:2; PSA 104:30

He performs miraculous deeds

ACT 8:39 See also 1KI 18:12; 2KI 2:16; MAT 12:28–12:38 parallels MRK 3:22–3:30, LUK 11:14–11:23

The Holy Spirit exercises his rule in the lives of individuals

Jesus Christ is led by the Spirit

MAT 4:1 parallels MRK 1:12, LUK 4:1

God's people are led by the Spirit

GAL 5:18 See also 1CH 28:12; PSA 143:10; MAT 10:20; LUK 2:27; JHN 16:13; ACT 8:29, ACT 20:22

God's people are restrained by the Spirit

ACT 16:6–16:7

God's people are given victory by the Spirit

JDG 3:10 See also JDG 14:6, JDG 14:19, JDG 15:14

The Holy Spirit's sovereignty is confirmed by the greatness of his attributes

PSA 139:7 His presence; ; 1CO 2:4 His power; ; 1CO 2:10–2:11 His knowledge

title: 3050: Holy Spirit, wisdom of
content:
The quality of being discerning and perceptive which is a characteristic of the Holy Spirit. The Holy Spirit is the source of human wisdom.

Those who knew and can know the wisdom of the Holy Spirit

Joshua

DEU 34:9 Either a wise human spirit or the Spirit of God who gives wisdom (NIV footnote).

The Messiah

ISA 11:2

Jesus Christ

LUK 2:40 See also COL 2:9 “fulness” includes wisdom.

Christians

EPH 1:17

The Holy Spirit is the source of wisdom

JOB 32:6–32:9; ACT 6:9–6:10; 1CO 2:12–2:14 1KI 3:8–3:9, 1KI 3:12, 1KI 4:29–4:34, 1KI 10:1, 1KI 10:23–10:24; PRO 2:6; ; DAN 5:10–5:16 Daniel's wisdom; ; ACT 6:3 the choosing of the Seven; ; ACT 15:28; ; 1CO 2:11, 1CO 12:8; ; COL 1:9

The Holy Spirit as teacher

JHN 14:26 See also NEH 9:20; MAT 10:19–10:20 parallels MRK 13:11, LUK 12:12; 1JN 2:27

title: 3110: Holy Spirit, titles and names of
content:
The names and terms for the Holy Spirit relate to his power, activity and presence in the world, often indicating the nature of his actions or the gifts which he conveys to believers.

Titles relating the Holy Spirit to God

The Spirit of God

GEN 1:2 See also GEN 41:38; 1SA 10:10, 1SA 19:20, 1SA 19:23; ROM 8:9; 1CO 6:11; 2CO 3:3; EPH 4:30; PHP 3:3

The Spirit of the LORD

The Spirit empowered the judges to deliver the people: JDG 3:10, JDG 6:34, JDG 11:29, JDG 13:25, JDG 14:6, JDG 14:19, JDG 15:14; 1SA 10:6, 1SA 16:13; ; 2SA 23:2; ISA 11:2, ISA 61:1; 2CO 3:17

Forms of reference to the Spirit of God

My Spirit

GEN 6:3; ; ISA 30:1, ISA 59:21; ; JOL 2:28–2:29; ; HAG 2:5; ; ZEC 4:6, ZEC 6:8; ; MAT 12:18

His Spirit

ISA 34:16, ISA 63:10–63:11; ; ZEC 7:12; ; ROM 8:11; ; EPH 2:22

Your Spirit

NEH 9:20, NEH 9:30; ; PSA 51:11, PSA 139:7, PSA 143:10

The Spirit of your Father

MAT 10:20

The promised Holy Spirit

ACT 2:33; ; EPH 1:13

The Spirit of him who raised Jesus from the dead

ROM 8:11

The spirit of the gods

Seen from a pagan viewpoint: DAN 4:8–4:9, DAN 4:18, DAN 5:11, DAN 5:14

Titles relating the Holy Spirit to God the Son

GAL 4:6 See also ACT 16:7; ROM 8:9; PHP 1:19; 1PE 1:11

Titles revealing the Holy Spirit's essential nature

The Spirit of truth

JHN 16:13 See also JHN 14:17, JHN 15:26; 1JN 4:6

The Spirit of holiness

ROM 1:4

The Spirit of life

ROM 8:2

The Spirit of glory

1PE 4:14

The eternal Spirit

HEB 9:14

Titles showing the nature of the Holy Spirit's activity

The Counsellor

JHN 14:26, JHN 15:26

The Spirit of wisdom and understanding

ISA 11:2 See also DEU 34:9; EPH 1:17

The Spirit of grace and supplication

ZEC 12:10

The Spirit of sonship

ROM 8:15

The Spirit of judgment and fire

ISA 4:4

title: 3120: Holy Spirit, descriptions of
content:
Though himself invisible, the Holy Spirit may appear in symbolic form and his activities may be described metaphorically.

The dove

MAT 3:16 parallels MRK 1:10, LUK 3:22 See also JHN 1:32

The wind or breath

ACT 2:2 This phenomenon accompanied the filling with the Spirit. See also EZK 37:9–37:14 The same Hebrew word is here translated “breath”, “wind” and “spirit”; JHN 3:8, JHN 20:22

The fire

ACT 2:3–2:4 See also ISA 4:4; MAT 3:11–3:12 parallel LUK 3:16–3:17; 1TH 5:19

The oil of anointing

ISA 61:1; ; ACT 10:38

Water

JHN 7:37–7:39 See also ISA 44:3; JHN 4:14; 1CO 12:13

A seal

EPH 1:13 See also EPH 4:30

A deposit

2CO 1:21–1:22; ; EPH 1:14

A guide or shepherd

ISA 63:14 See also ISA 34:16

A voice

HEB 3:7–3:11 See also EZK 2:2; MAT 10:19–10:20 parallel MRK 13:11; JHN 16:13

God's gift

ACT 2:38 See also ACT 1:4, ACT 8:20, ACT 10:45, ACT 11:17

The power of God

LUK 1:35 See also LUK 5:17, LUK 6:19, LUK 24:49

title: 3130: Holy Spirit, as Counsellor
content:
The Holy Spirit is the one who comforts, advises and strengthens Christians, drawing them closer to Jesus Christ.

The Holy Spirit is Counsellor in addition to Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ is a Counsellor

1JN 2:1 One Greek word “Parakletos” underlies this phrase which is primarily a legal term. It can also bear the meaning “comforter”.

The Spirit is another Counsellor

JHN 14:16–14:17 The word “another” means “another of the same kind”. After the ascension, the Spirit is to assume the ministry of Jesus Christ.

The Counsellor is the gift of the exalted Christ

JHN 16:7 See also JHN 7:38–7:39; ACT 2:33

The Counsellor comforts and reassures believers

JHN 14:16–14:18; ; ACT 11:12 Peter is reassured about going to the house of Cornelius; ; ROM 8:16 Believers are reassured that they are God's children.

The Counsellor strengthens and equips the church

EPH 3:16 See also ACT 4:31 The Jerusalem church is strengthened in the face of opposition; ACT 9:31 the church in Judea, Galilee and Samaria; ROM 8:26

The Counsellor teaches and instructs believers

The Spirit reminds the disciples of Jesus Christ's teaching

JHN 14:26 See also JHN 16:14; 1JN 5:6–5:8

The Spirit teaches the church further truth

JHN 16:13 See also 1CO 2:9–2:10; 1JN 2:27

The Counsellor helps the church in its mission

The Spirit testifies to Jesus Christ and helps the church to do likewise

JHN 15:26–15:27 See also ACT 1:8

The Spirit convicts the unbelieving world

JHN 16:8–16:11; ; 1CO 14:24–14:25

Examples of the Spirit's help in witness and mission

ACT 4:8 Peter before the Sanhedrin; ; 1CO 2:3–2:4 Paul in Corinth; ; 1TH 1:5 Paul and Silas in Thessalonica

The Counsellor draws believers closer to Jesus Christ

EPH 3:16–3:17 See also JHN 14:23; ROM 8:9–8:11

title: 3140: Holy Spirit, as teacher
content:
Having instructed God's people in the OT and Jesus Christ's disciples in the NT, the Spirit of truth continues to reveal the truth of God to believers.

The Holy Spirit as teacher in the OT

The Spirit teaches on practical matters

1CH 28:12–28:13 See also EXO 31:2–31:6 parallel EXO 35:30–35:35; 1CH 28:19

The Spirit teaches the ways of God

PSA 143:10 See also NEH 9:20, NEH 9:30; ISA 48:16–48:17

The Spirit teaches about the salvation which is to come in Jesus Christ

1PE 1:10–1:11 See also HEB 9:8, HEB 10:15–10:17

The Holy Spirit empowers Jesus Christ's teaching

LUK 4:14–4:15 See also JHN 3:34; ACT 1:2

The Holy Spirit teaches Jesus Christ's disciples

The Spirit will teach the disciples what to say when persecuted

MAT 10:19–10:20 parallels MRK 13:11, LUK 12:11–12:12 See also ACT 4:8, ACT 6:10

Jesus Christ promises the Spirit's teaching ministry

JHN 14:26 See also JHN 14:16, JHN 16:13–16:15

The Spirit continues to teach believers

1JN 2:20 See also 1JN 2:27

The Spirit teaches the churches

REV 2:7, REV 2:11, REV 2:17, REV 2:29, REV 3:6, REV 3:13, REV 3:22

The Spirit teaches within believers' hearts, in contrast to the written code

EZK 36:27; ; ROM 2:29; ; 2CO 3:7–3:8

The Holy Spirit's teaching centres on Jesus Christ

JHN 16:14–16:15

The Holy Spirit inspires Scripture for teaching

2TI 3:16

title: 3200: Holy Spirit, ministry and work of
content: • 3203: Holy Spirit, and assurance

title: 3203: Holy Spirit, and assurance
content:
The Holy Spirit assures believers of their standing in Christ and their eternal salvation.

The Holy Spirit makes an individual a member of Christ

ROM 8:9 See also 1CO 2:12, 1CO 6:11, 1CO 6:17; 1TH 4:8

The Holy Spirit assures believers that they have been born anew

ROM 2:29 The true sign of belonging to God is not any outward mark on the body, but the regenerating power of the Spirit within. See also JHN 3:5–3:6; ROM 7:6; 2CO 3:6; GAL 4:29; TIT 3:5–3:7

The Holy Spirit assures believers that they are God's children

ROM 8:14–8:16 See also GAL 4:6

The Holy Spirit assures believers that God loves them

ROM 5:5

The Holy Spirit assures believers that Jesus Christ lives in them and they in him

1JN 4:13 See also 1CO 12:13; 1JN 3:24

The Holy Spirit assures believers that God's power is within them

GAL 3:5 See also 1CO 2:4–2:5

The fruit of the Spirit is evidence of the believer's standing in Christ

MAT 7:15–7:20 Spiritual gifts alone are not a sufficient basis for assurance. See also ACT 13:52; ROM 8:6, ROM 14:17; 2CO 6:6; GAL 5:16–5:25; 1TH 1:6

The Holy Spirit assures believers of the significance of Jesus Christ

1CO 2:10–2:12 See also 2CO 3:14–3:17; EPH 3:5

The Holy Spirit is the first instalment of the believer's inheritance in the kingdom of God

2CO 1:21–1:22 See also EPH 1:13–1:14, EPH 4:30

The Holy Spirit assures believers of their final victory in Christ

2CO 5:5 See also ROM 8:11, ROM 8:15–8:17, ROM 8:23; EPH 1:14

title: 3206: Holy Spirit, and grace
content: See 6670: grace, and Holy Spirit

title: 3209: Holy Spirit, and love
content:
Heartfelt concern and steadfast practical care is part of the evidence of the Holy Spirit's presence in the lives of believers.

The Holy Spirit's work and fruit includes the characteristic of love

GAL 5:22 See also ACT 9:31; ROM 15:30; COL 1:8; 1TH 4:8–4:10

The Holy Spirit fills believers with the love of God

ROM 5:5 See also EZK 11:19; EPH 3:16–3:19

The Holy Spirit enables believers to live with one another in love

EPH 4:2–4:3 See also 2CO 6:6; GAL 5:14–5:16, GAL 5:25–5:26; PHP 2:1–2:2

The gift of the Holy Spirit results in practical love

ACT 4:31–4:35

Love is essential in the exercise of the gifts of the Holy Spirit

1CO 13:1–13:13 See also 1CO 14:1, 1CO 14:12, 1CO 14:26

resource.label: 8463: priority, of faith, hope and love

title: 3212: Holy Spirit, and mission
content:
The Holy Spirit directs and empowers believers in their missionary tasks, bearing witness to Jesus Christ and preparing the hearts of men and women to respond to him in faith.

The Holy Spirit equips God's people

He inspired the prophets

NEH 9:30; ; JOB 32:8; ; ISA 48:16; ; MIC 3:8; ; HEB 1:1

He empowered Jesus Christ as Messiah

ISA 11:2, ISA 42:1; ; MAT 3:16 parallels MRK 1:10, LUK 3:21–3:22, JHN 1:32–1:34; ; ACT 10:38

He empowered the first Christians

ACT 1:8, ACT 2:16–2:17, ACT 8:17, ACT 10:44, ACT 11:15, ACT 19:6 The outpouring of God's Spirit begins to extend beyond the Jews.

He empowers God's people today

JHN 14:16; ; ROM 8:26–8:27; ; 1CO 12:3–12:7

The Holy Spirit accomplishes the mission of God

He convicts of sin

JHN 16:8 See also ACT 2:37; 1CO 14:24–14:25

He directs mission

ACT 13:2 See also ACT 8:29, ACT 10:19–10:20, ACT 13:4, ACT 16:6–16:10

He inspires witnesses and speakers

ACT 6:9–6:10 See also MAT 10:19–10:20 parallels MRK 13:11, LUK 21:14–21:15; ACT 2:14, ACT 4:31, ACT 11:15, ACT 13:9–13:10; 1TH 1:5; 1PE 1:12

He gives signs and wonders

ROM 15:18–15:19 See also JHN 1:50; ACT 5:13; 2CO 12:12; GAL 3:5; HEB 2:4

The Holy Spirit's missionary activity is ongoing

He cares for God's people involved in mission

JHN 16:13, JHN 16:7; ; ACT 15:28; ; 1PE 5:7

He sanctifies God's people

ROM 8:10, ROM 8:14, ROM 15:16; ; 2TH 2:13

He encourages God's people

ROM 8:16, ROM 8:27; ; 2TH 2:17; ; 1JN 3:24, 1JN 4:13

title: 3215: Holy Spirit, and peace
content:
The Holy Spirit brings a sense of well-being, contentment and wholeness to believers whatever their outward circumstances. Peace is therefore an indication of the Holy Spirit's presence.

Peace is part of the nature of the Holy Spirit

The Spirit is likened to a dove, the symbol of peace

MAT 3:16 parallels MRK 1:10, LUK 3:22, JHN 1:32

As part of the Godhead the Spirit is the God of peace

See also PHP 4:9; 1TH 5:23; HEB 13:20

Peace is a resource that the Holy Spirit gives to believers

ROM 14:17 See also JHN 14:15–14:19 The Greek word “parakletos” means “one called alongside”, hence “comforter” or “advocate”; ROM 15:13

The fruit of the Spirit includes peace

GAL 5:22–5:25 See also ROM 8:6

The Holy Spirit brings peace to God's people concerning their circumstances

He changes situations to bring peace

ISA 32:14–32:18 See also ISA 63:14; ACT 9:31

He brings peace of mind to those facing difficult situations

PHP 1:19 See also HAG 2:5; JHN 16:5–16:21, JHN 20:21–20:22

The Holy Spirit brings peaceful relationships

Between believers

EPH 4:3

Between believers and God the Father

ROM 8:1–8:2 See also ROM 8:11, ROM 8:14–8:17 The result of the Spirit's work is contrasted with fear, the opposite of peace.

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 3218: Holy Spirit, and praise
content:
The Holy Spirit inspires believers to extol, worship and thank God. His aim is to glorify the Father and the Son.

The promise that the Holy Spirit will bring praise

ISA 61:1–61:3

The Holy Spirit inspires praise

True praise must be inspired by the Spirit

JHN 4:23–4:24 See also PHP 3:3

The Spirit prompts praise

2SA 23:1–23:2 LUK 1:67–1:68, LUK 2:27–2:28; LUK 10:21 The Spirit prompts Jesus Christ to praise the Father.

Evidence of the Holy Spirit's work inspires praise

The Spirit's convicting work inspires praise

1CO 14:24–14:25

The Spirit's converting work inspires praise

ACT 11:15–11:18 See also 2TH 2:13

The Holy Spirit activates the church's praise

EPH 5:18–5:20 See also ACT 10:44–10:46; COL 3:16–3:17

title: 3221: Holy Spirit, and prayer
content:
The Holy Spirit intercedes for God's people and also prompts their petitions, supplications and thanksgivings.

The Holy Spirit enables God's children to address him as ``Abba''

ROM 8:15 See also GAL 4:6

The role of the Holy Spirit in prayer

He prompts supplication

ZEC 12:10 “a spirit” (NIV text) or “the Spirit” (NIV footnote).

He inspires prayers of praise

LUK 10:21 See also EPH 5:18–5:20

He inspires praying in tongues

ACT 2:4 See also ACT 10:44–10:46, ACT 19:6; 1CO 14:14–14:18

He intercedes for believers

ROM 8:26–8:27

The evidence of the Holy Spirit's work prompts praise and thanks

1TH 1:2–1:5 See also ACT 11:15–11:18

Believers are exhorted to pray in the Spirit

EPH 6:18–6:20 See also JUD 1:20

The love of the Holy Spirit is a motive for prayer

ROM 15:30–15:32 See also LUK 11:13; ACT 8:14–8:17

title: 3224: Holy Spirit, and preaching
content:
True Christian preaching is grounded in the word of God and applied by the Holy Spirit to its audience.

The Holy Spirit is identified with the proclaimed word of God

EPH 6:17 See also JHN 6:63

The Holy Spirit sends God's messengers

ACT 13:4–13:5

The Holy Spirit equips, inspires and instructs God's messengers

He inspires the OT prophets

MIC 3:8 See also ISA 61:1–61:2; EZK 2:2–2:5; 2PE 1:21

He inspires Jesus Christ's teaching ministry

LUK 4:18–4:21 See also ISA 61:1–61:2; MAT 12:18; ISA 42:1; JHN 3:34

The apostles preach in the Spirit's power

1CO 2:4–2:5 See also LUK 24:45–24:49; ACT 4:8–4:12 Peter; ACT 6:10 Stephen; ACT 13:4–13:5 Paul and Barnabas; 1TH 1:5; 1PE 1:12

The Holy Spirit teaches and applies the preached word of God to its hearers

JHN 14:26 See also JHN 16:15; 1CO 2:13

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of
3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 3227: Holy Spirit, and prophecy
content: See 1427: prophecy

title: 3230: Holy Spirit, and regeneration
content:
God's Spirit works to bring the gift of new birth and renewal to those who have been called to faith.

The Holy Spirit's work is promised

EZK 36:26–36:27 See also EZK 37:14

Jesus Christ proclaims the Holy Spirit's work

JHN 3:5–3:8

The Holy Spirit's work brings salvation

TIT 3:5–3:6 “washing” (apparently a reference to baptism) signifies regeneration. See also EPH 5:25–5:27

Regeneration is a creative work of God's Spirit alone

It cannot be achieved by human means

JHN 6:63 See also JHN 1:13, JHN 3:6

It cannot be achieved through the works of the law

2CO 3:6 See also ROM 2:29, ROM 7:6, ROM 8:2; GAL 3:2

resource.label: 3296: Holy Spirit, work in the world

title: 3233: Holy Spirit, and sanctification
content:
The work of the Holy Spirit in enabling believers to lead holy lives, dedicated to the service of God and conformed to his likeness.

The Spirit of holiness is promised

MAT 3:11 parallel LUK 3:16 “fire” implies the Holy Spirit's work of purification and judgment.

Sanctification is a special work of the Holy Spirit

ROM 15:16 See also 1CO 6:11; GAL 5:5; 1PE 1:2

The Holy Spirit requires believers to be sanctified

2TH 2:13 Sanctification is a necessary part of being a Christian. See also 1CO 6:18–6:19

The Holy Spirit enables believers to be sanctified

ROM 8:4 See also ROM 8:13; EPH 5:18

The Holy Spirit produces sanctification

GAL 5:22–5:23 See also ROM 14:17; 2TI 1:7

The process of sanctification

The Holy Spirit makes believers more like Jesus Christ

2CO 3:18

The Holy Spirit helps mortify sinful human nature

ROM 8:13 See also GAL 5:17

The Holy Spirit is opposed to natural desires

GAL 5:16–5:17 See also ROM 8:5–8:9; JUD 1:19

Examples of people sanctified by the Holy Spirit

Joshua: NUM 27:18; ; DEU 34:9; LUK 2:25 Simeon; ACT 6:3, ACT 6:5; ACT 11:24 Barnabas; ; 2CO 6:6 Paul and his companions

title: 3236: Holy Spirit, and Scripture
content:
The Holy Spirit inspired the original writing of all the Scriptures, and illumines their meaning to believers.

The Holy Spirit inspired the writing of Scripture

The Spirit inspired the writers of the OT

2SA 23:1–23:2 See also NEH 9:30; EZK 2:2, EZK 11:24–11:25; MIC 3:8; ZEC 7:12

The NT recognises OT writings as inspired by the Holy Spirit

2TI 3:16 “God-breathed” means “breathed out by God” rather than “inspired”; 2PE 1:20–1:21 See also MAT 22:43 parallel MRK 12:36; ACT 1:16, ACT 4:25, ACT 28:25–28:27; HEB 3:7–3:11, HEB 10:15–10:17; 1PE 1:11

The Spirit inspired the writers of the NT

EPH 3:4–3:5 This revelation by the Spirit probably includes the apostles' understanding of the gospel and their preaching as well as the writings of the NT. See also 1CO 7:40; REV 2:7, REV 2:11, REV 2:17, REV 2:29, REV 3:6, REV 3:13, REV 3:22

The Holy Spirit illumines the meaning of Scripture

1CO 2:12–2:16 “what God has freely given” refers to “God's secret wisdom” (verse 7), the “deep things of God” (verse 10) and the “thoughts of God” (verse 11). See also JHN 14:26, JHN 16:13–16:15; 2CO 3:14–3:17; HEB 9:8; 1JN 2:20, 1JN 2:27

The relationship between the word and the Holy Spirit

The association of God's Spirit and breath with his word(s)

PSA 33:6; ; ISA 59:21; ; JHN 3:34, JHN 6:63; ; EPH 6:17 “word” here probably means “the gospel” rather than Scripture as a whole.

The Spirit is essential for obeying God's law

ROM 2:29 Reliance on the letter of the law alone is useless. See also ROM 7:6; 2CO 3:6; GAL 3:2, GAL 3:5, GAL 5:18

The Spirit enables believers to fulfil the law

ROM 8:4 See also GAL 5:4–5:5

title: 3239: Holy Spirit, anointing of
content:
“Anoint” means to set someone apart, to authorise and equip him or her for a task of spiritual importance. Jesus Christ is set apart by the work of the Holy Spirit for his ministry of preaching, healing and deliverance. The Holy Spirit sets Christians apart for their ministry in Christ's name.

Jesus Christ anointed by the Holy Spirit

The Messiah's anointing is predicted

ISA 61:1–61:3 “Messiah” means “anointed one”.

The Spirit anoints Jesus Christ at the start of his ministry

MAT 3:16 parallels MRK 1:10, LUK 3:22, JHN 1:32ACT 10:38

Jesus Christ declares his anointing

LUK 4:18–4:21 See also ISA 61:1–61:2

The first Christians declare Jesus Christ's anointing

ACT 4:26–4:27

Evidence pointing to the Spirit's anointing in Jesus Christ's ministry

See also MAT 4:23–4:25, MAT 7:28–7:29, MAT 12:28; LUK 4:14–4:15, LUK 5:17, LUK 6:19, LUK 7:14–7:15

Believers anointed by the Holy Spirit

The Spirit anoints God's chosen people

2SA 23:1–23:2 Samuel

Anointing enables Christians to stand firm

2CO 1:21–1:22

Anointing guards Christians against falsehood

1JN 2:20, 1JN 2:27

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 3242: Holy Spirit, baptism with
content:
A divine act, promised by John the Baptist and by Jesus Christ, whereby the Holy Spirit initiates Christians into realised union and communion with the glorified Jesus Christ, thus equipping and enabling them for sanctity and service.

Baptism with the Holy Spirit promised

John the Baptist anticipates baptism with the Spirit

JHN 1:33 parallels MAT 3:11, MRK 1:8, LUK 3:16

Jesus Christ promises baptism with the Spirit

ACT 1:4–1:5 See also LUK 24:49; ACT 1:8

The gift of the Holy Spirit followed Jesus Christ's glorification

ACT 2:33 See also JHN 7:39

Instances of baptism with the Holy Spirit

ACT 2:2–2:4 at Pentecost; ACT 8:15–8:17, ACT 10:44–10:47, ACT 19:6; ACT 10:46–10:47, ACT 11:15–11:17, ACT 15:8

The gift of the Holy Spirit is for all believers at the outset of their Christian lives

ACT 2:38–2:39 See also ACT 2:16–2:18; JOL 2:28–2:29; GAL 3:2–3:5

This gift of the Holy Spirit links believers together in the one body of Christ

1CO 12:13

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 3245: Holy Spirit, blasphemy against
content:
The denial of the action of the Holy Spirit in the ministry of Jesus Christ, or the attribution of his works to demonic influence.

Jesus Christ warns against blasphemy

MRK 3:29 parallels MAT 12:31–12:32, LUK 12:10 See also 1JN 5:16

Blasphemy as erroneously attributing the work of the Holy Spirit to demonic influence

Jesus Christ is accused of being possessed by demons

MRK 3:22 parallels MAT 12:24, LUK 11:15 See also MAT 10:25; JHN 7:20, JHN 8:48, JHN 8:52, JHN 10:20

Jesus Christ is accused of casting out demons by demonic power

MAT 9:34 See also MAT 12:24 parallels MRK 3:22, LUK 11:15

Jesus Christ refutes these charges

MAT 12:25–12:29 parallels MRK 3:23–3:27, LUK 11:17–11:22

Blasphemy as acting in ways which deny the Holy Spirit's work

ACT 5:3 See also ISA 63:10; 1TH 5:19; HEB 10:26–10:31

title: 3248: Holy Spirit, conviction of
content:
The Holy Spirit convinces human beings of sin and of the reality of forgiveness through Jesus Christ.

The Holy Spirit convicts the world of sin and its consequences

JHN 16:8–16:11

Ways in which the Holy Spirit brings conviction

Through preaching

ACT 2:37 See also ACT 16:14; 1TH 1:5

Through the exercise of spiritual gifts

1CO 14:24–14:25

The reality of the Holy Spirit's conviction in the lives of believers

He brings illumination

EPH 1:17–1:18 See also 1CO 2:8–2:10, 1CO 2:14–2:16

He gives assurance about their new relationship with God

ROM 8:15–8:16 See also GAL 4:6

He convicts of the need for constant change

ROM 8:5–8:9 See also GAL 5:16–5:23

resource.label: 3296: Holy Spirit, work in the world

title: 3251: Holy Spirit, filling with
content:
To be filled with the Holy Spirit is to be energised and controlled by the third person of the Godhead in such a way that under the acknowledged lordship of Jesus Christ the full presence and power of God are experienced. Spirit-filling leads to renewal, obedience, boldness in testimony and an arresting quality in believers' lives.

People filled with the Holy Spirit before the ministry of Jesus Christ

In the OT

EXO 31:3 Bezalel filled with the Spirit for artwork on the tabernacle; ; DEU 34:9 Joshua filled with the Spirit to succeed Moses

In the events surrounding the birth of John the Baptist

LUK 1:15 John the Baptist filled with the Spirit from birth; ; LUK 1:41 Elizabeth filled with the Spirit and speaks words of praise; ; LUK 1:67 Zechariah prophesies about the life of John and God's salvation

Jesus Christ is full of the Holy Spirit

LUK 4:1 See also LUK 10:21; JHN 3:34; ACT 10:38

NT terminology

ACT 1:5, ACT 2:4, ACT 10:47 While these terms are virtually synonymous when used of initial experiences of the Spirit, “filled” is also used to designate subsequent experiences and renewings of the same divine power, while the other terms are not.

``Be filled with the Spirit'': an apostolic command

EPH 5:18 The present tense of the verb implies the need to be regularly filled and re-filled with the Spirit.

``Filled with the Spirit'': a conscious experience of God's power

ACT 2:4 See also ACT 4:31, ACT 9:17

``Full of the Spirit'': a consistent quality of Christian character

ACT 6:3–6:5 See also ACT 11:24, ACT 13:52

``Filled with the Spirit'': the inspiration to speak words of witness, challenge or rebuke

ACT 4:8 Peter testifying before the Sanhedrin; ; ACT 7:55 Stephen testifying to the Sanhedrin, seeing the glory of God; ; ACT 13:9 Paul rebuking Elymas

Characteristics of the Spirit-filled life

The Spirit of Christ should rule believers' lives

ROM 8:4–8:6 See also GAL 5:16, GAL 5:25

The Spirit produces fruit of Christlike character

ROM 15:13; ; 2CO 6:6; ; GAL 5:22–5:23

The Spirit brings liberty

ROM 7:6 See also 2CO 3:17; GAL 5:1

Being filled with the Spirit often leads to words of praise

ACT 2:4 See also ACT 4:31, ACT 10:44–10:46, ACT 19:6; EPH 5:18–5:20

Characteristics linked with the fulness of the Spirit

Skill: EXO 31:3, EXO 35:31; DEU 34:9; ; ACT 6:3; LUK 10:21; ; ACT 13:52; ACT 6:5, ACT 11:24

title: 3254: Holy Spirit, fruit of
content:
The living presence of the Holy Spirit in believers leads to Christlike virtues within them, just as a living tree will bear good fruit.

God expects his people to bear spiritual fruit

ISA 5:4 See also MAT 7:12–7:20 “fruit” is a term used to indicate the produce or outcome of a person's life; JHN 15:8

The fruit of the Spirit leads to believers becoming Christlike

The gift of the Spirit begins this lifelong process

2CO 3:18

Christlike qualities are contrasted with sinful ones

GAL 5:16–5:17 See also ROM 8:5–8:14; 1CO 12:7; GAL 6:8; EPH 5:8–5:16; COL 3:1–3:17

The fruit of Spirit-filled living

GAL 5:22–5:23 “fruit” is singular, emphasising that these qualities are mutually dependent aspects of Christian living. See also EPH 5:9

Examples of the fruit of the Spirit

Love

ROM 5:5; ; 1CO 13:1–13:13

Joy

PHP 1:18–1:19; ; 1TH 1:6

Peace

ROM 8:6, ROM 14:17

Patience

HEB 6:12; ; JAS 5:7–5:11

Kindness

2CO 6:6; ; COL 3:12; ; 2PE 1:7

Goodness

ROM 15:14; ; 2PE 1:5

Faithfulness

1CO 10:13 God's faithfulness is also here presented as the reason for faithfulness; ; 3JN 1:3

Gentleness

MAT 11:29–11:30 Jesus Christ displays such gentleness; ; 1TH 2:7

Self-control

2TI 3:3; ; 2PE 1:6

Other evidence of the Holy Spirit's activity

ROM 14:17 righteousness; ROM 15:13; ; GAL 5:5; EPH 1:17 wisdom; ; EPH 5:18 temperance

The evidence of the fruit of the Spirit is a result of divine activity, not of human effort

1CO 3:9, 1CO 3:16; ; GAL 2:20

title: 3257: Holy Spirit, gift of
content:
In the OT the gift of the Holy Spirit was restricted to individuals for particular tasks, but at Pentecost and on subsequent occasions, the Holy Spirit is given to all believers.

The gift of the Holy Spirit in the OT

The Spirit is given to individuals for specific tasks

1SA 10:6 to Saul, for kingship; ; EXO 31:2–31:5 to Bezalel, for craftsmanship

The gift of the Spirit can be withdrawn

1SA 16:14; PSA 51:11 See also JDG 16:20; 1KI 22:24

The gift of the Holy Spirit is foretold

He is promised by John the Baptist

MAT 3:11 parallels MRK 1:8, LUK 3:16, JHN 1:33

He is promised by Jesus Christ

LUK 24:49; ; JHN 7:37–7:39, JHN 14:16, JHN 14:26, JHN 15:26, JHN 16:7; ; ACT 1:4–1:5, ACT 1:8, ACT 11:16

The gift of the Holy Spirit before Pentecost

Individuals filled with the Spirit

LUK 1:15 See also LUK 1:41, LUK 1:67, LUK 2:25–2:27

Jesus Christ receives the gift of the Spirit

MAT 3:16 parallels MRK 1:10, LUK 3:22, JHN 1:32

Jesus Christ bids his disciples receive the Holy Spirit

See also JHN 20:22

The gift of the Holy Spirit at Pentecost and after

At Pentecost

ACT 2:2–2:4 See also ACT 2:33

The Spirit was given on later occasions

ACT 10:44 See also ACT 8:15–8:17, ACT 9:17, ACT 19:6; GAL 3:5; HEB 2:4

The gift of the Spirit is for all believers

ACT 2:38–2:39 See also ACT 2:17, ACT 5:32, ACT 10:45, ACT 11:17, ACT 15:8; ROM 8:9; GAL 3:14; 1TH 4:8; HEB 2:4

God's willingness to give the Spirit

LUK 11:13 See also JHN 3:34

God's purposes require the gift of the Holy Spirit

To give assurance of acceptance by God

ACT 15:8 See also ACT 10:47, ACT 11:17; 1JN 3:24, 1JN 4:13

To convict the world of sin

JHN 16:7–16:8; ; ACT 2:37

To provide spiritual gifts

ACT 2:4, ACT 10:45–10:46, ACT 19:6; ; 1CO 12:4–12:11

To work miracles, signs and wonders

ROM 15:18–15:19; ; GAL 3:5; ; HEB 2:4

To give boldness in preaching and witnessing

ACT 1:8 See also ACT 4:31, ACT 5:32

To cultivate the fruit of the Spirit

ROM 14:17 See also ROM 5:5, ROM 15:13; GAL 5:16

To sanctify the believer

2TH 2:13; ; 1PE 1:2

title: 3260: Holy Spirit, gifts of
content: See 7966: spiritual gifts

title: 3263: Holy Spirit, guidance of
content:
The Holy Spirit gives guidance to groups and individuals facing challenges, decisions and difficulties. Such guidance, however given, effectively specifies the will of God in situations of choice within the biblically established guidelines of righteousness.

Knowing divine guidance is associated with the presence of the Holy Spirit

ISA 63:11–63:14 See also 1KI 18:12; PSA 139:7–139:10

The Holy Spirit guides Jesus Christ in his ministry

LUK 4:1 parallels MAT 4:1, MRK 1:12 See also ACT 10:37–10:38

The Holy Spirit guides the church in its corporate decisions

ACT 13:2 See also ACT 15:28

The Holy Spirit's guidance of believers

The Holy Spirit guides individual believers

ROM 8:14 See also GAL 5:18

He guides through the word of God, spiritually understood and kept before the mind

ISA 59:21

He guides in prayer

ROM 8:26–8:27

He guides through spiritual gifts

1CO 12:7–12:11

He guides in Christian living

ROM 8:14; ; GAL 5:25

He guides in witness and evangelism

MRK 13:11 parallels MAT 10:19–10:20, LUK 12:11–12:12ACT 8:29 See also 1CO 2:13

He guides in ministry

ACT 16:6–16:7, ACT 20:22

He guides into a knowledge of truth

JHN 16:13

resource.label: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ

title: 3266: Holy Spirit, in creation
content:
The Holy Spirit was active with the Father and the Word in creation. He is the active power of God present within creation.

The Holy Spirit is involved in creative activity

GEN 1:2 See also JOB 26:13; PSA 33:6 The word translated “breath” is the same word that is used for “Spirit”.

The Holy Spirit is the breath of life throughout creation

GEN 2:7 See also JOB 12:10, JOB 32:8, JOB 33:4, JOB 34:14–34:15; PSA 104:30

The Holy Spirit is present everywhere in creation

PSA 139:7–139:8

The Holy Spirit controls nature and history

ISA 34:16 See also ISA 40:7

The Holy Spirit enables creative achievement

EXO 31:1–31:5 See also EXO 35:30–35:35

title: 3269: Holy Spirit, in life of Jesus Christ
content:
From the moment of his conception Jesus Christ was empowered by the Holy Spirit. Christ's possession of the Holy Spirit was demonstrated publicly at several points in his ministry. After his resurrection the Holy Spirit demonstrated him to be the Son of God.

OT prophecies of the Holy Spirit in Jesus Christ's life

ISA 11:2 See also PSA 45:2; ISA 42:1, ISA 61:1; ZEC 12:10

Jesus Christ was conceived by the Holy Spirit

LUK 1:35 See also MAT 1:18, MAT 1:20

The Holy Spirit at Jesus Christ's baptism

MAT 3:16 parallels MRK 1:10, LUK 3:22, JHN 1:32

Jesus Christ lived by the Holy Spirit

LUK 10:21 See also MAT 4:1 parallels MRK 1:12, LUK 4:1; LUK 1:80; 1PE 3:18

Jesus Christ's ministry was empowered by the Holy Spirit

HEB 9:14

In preaching

LUK 4:18 See also ISA 61:1; MAT 12:18; LUK 4:14–4:15, LUK 4:21; JHN 3:34; ACT 1:2

In working miracles

ACT 10:38 See also MAT 12:15–12:18, MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20; LUK 5:17

Jesus Christ was vindicated by the Holy Spirit

ROM 1:4 See also 1TI 3:16; 1PE 3:18

Jesus Christ promises the Holy Spirit to his disciples

LUK 11:13 See also MAT 10:20 parallel MRK 13:11; LUK 12:12; JHN 7:39, JHN 14:16–14:17, JHN 14:26, JHN 15:26, JHN 16:13–16:15

Jesus Christ gives the Holy Spirit to his disciples

JHN 20:22 See also ACT 2:33

resource.label: 2363: Jesus Christ, preaching and teaching of

title: 3272: Holy Spirit, in OT
content:
The OT portrays the Holy Spirit as being active in creation, in equipping individuals for skilled tasks and in inspiring prophecy and revelation.

The Holy Spirit as the presence of God

He is active in creation

GEN 1:2 See also JOB 33:4; PSA 104:30

He is present everywhere

PSA 139:7

His sovereignty

ISA 34:16 See also EZK 36:27, EZK 37:14

All life is dependent on the Spirit

JOB 34:14–34:15 NIV footnote at verse 14. See also GEN 6:3

He is present with God's people

ISA 63:11–63:14 See also NEH 9:20; HAG 2:5

The Holy Spirit equips individuals

For leadership

JDG 3:10 See also NUM 11:17, NUM 11:25–11:29 The gift of prophecy was given temporarily to the elders in order to establish them as leaders of God's people; NUM 27:18; DEU 34:9; JDG 6:34, JDG 11:29, JDG 13:25; 1SA 10:6, 1SA 11:6, 1SA 16:13

In bringing revelation

GEN 41:38–41:39 See also DAN 4:8–4:9, DAN 4:18, DAN 5:11, DAN 5:14

By empowering the prophet

EZK 2:2, EZK 3:12, EZK 3:14, EZK 3:24, EZK 8:3, EZK 11:24, EZK 43:5

By giving special strength

JDG 14:6, JDG 14:19, JDG 15:14

By guidance

PSA 143:10

In planning

1CH 28:12

In craftsmanship

EXO 31:3–31:5, EXO 35:31–35:35

In building

ZEC 4:6

The Holy Spirit inspires

Prophecy

2CH 24:20 See also NUM 24:2; 1SA 10:6, 1SA 10:10, 1SA 19:20, 1SA 19:23; 2CH 15:1, 2CH 20:14; ISA 48:16; EZK 11:5; MIC 3:8; 2PE 1:21

Teaching

NEH 9:20, NEH 9:30

Praise

2SA 23:1–23:2

A declaration of allegiance

1CH 12:18

Examples of the awareness of the Holy Spirit's activity

1KI 18:12; ; 2KI 2:16

Examples of grieving the Holy Spirit

ISA 63:10 See also GEN 6:3; 1SA 16:14; PSA 51:11, PSA 106:33–106:48; ISA 30:1; MIC 2:7; ZEC 7:12

Promises of the Holy Spirit for the future

The Spirit and the Messiah

ISA 11:2 See also ISA 42:1, ISA 61:1

The outpouring of the Spirit

JOL 2:28–2:29 See also ISA 32:15, ISA 44:3, ISA 59:21; EZK 36:27, EZK 37:14, EZK 39:29

title: 3275: Holy Spirit, in the church
content:
The church depends upon the activity of the Holy Spirit, without which its effective and faithful service is impossible.

The Holy Spirit forms the church

1CO 12:13 See also ACT 2:1–2:4, ACT 2:16–2:18; JOL 2:28–2:29; ACT 10:44–10:48; EPH 2:21–2:22

The Holy Spirit indwells the church

1CO 3:16 “you” here is plural and refers to the whole church. See also EZK 10:4; JHN 16:14; 1CO 6:19; 2CO 6:16; 1PE 4:14

The Holy Spirit enables the church to function as the body of Christ

1CO 12:7 See also EXO 31:1–31:5; NUM 11:24–11:27; 1SA 10:5–10:11; ROM 12:5; 1CO 12:8–12:11

The Holy Spirit enables Christian unity

EPH 4:3–4:4; ; PHP 2:1

The church worships, serves and speaks by the Holy Spirit

The church worships by the Spirit

ACT 2:11 See also JHN 4:24; ACT 10:45–10:46; ROM 8:15; 1CO 14:26–14:33; PHP 3:3

The church serves by the Spirit

ROM 12:6–12:8 See also 1CO 12:7–12:11; EPH 4:7–4:13

The church speaks by the Spirit

ACT 6:10 See also ACT 4:8; 1CO 14:24–14:25

The Holy Spirit communicates Jesus Christ's message to the church

REV 2:7 See also JHN 14:26, JHN 16:13; REV 2:11, REV 2:17, REV 2:29, REV 3:6, REV 3:13, REV 3:22

The Holy Spirit and the church's mission

The church fulfils its mission by the Spirit

ACT 1:8 See also JHN 15:26–15:27, JHN 20:21–20:23; ACT 4:31; GAL 3:3

The Spirit directs the church's missionary enterprise

ACT 13:2–13:3, ACT 15:28, ACT 16:6

title: 3278: Holy Spirit, indwelling of
content:
The Holy Spirit dwells within Jesus Christ and his disciples. Recognisable results in believers' lives include Christlikeness and the fruit of the Spirit.

The indwelling of the Holy Spirit in the OT

GEN 41:38; EXO 35:31 See also NUM 27:18; 1SA 10:6–10:7; ISA 59:21; HAG 2:5

The Holy Spirit indwells Jesus Christ

ISA 11:2; ; MAT 12:18; ; ISA 42:1; ; LUK 4:18; ; ISA 61:1; ; ACT 10:38

The indwelling of the Holy Spirit is promised to believers

JHN 14:17 See also EZK 36:27, EZK 37:14

The Holy Spirit indwells believers

2TI 1:14 1CO 3:16, 1CO 6:19; EPH 2:22; ; 1JN 2:27, 1JN 3:24

Results of the Holy Spirit's indwelling in believers

title: 3287: Holy Spirit, sealing of
content:
Based on the practice of sealing letters, documents and property, the Holy Spirit is described as being a seal on the lives of God's people, affirming their Christian character, marking them out as belonging to God and guarding them from the world and the devil.

The Holy Spirit's seal on the life of Jesus Christ

JHN 6:27 The Greek implies a once-and-for-all sealing at a particular time in the past and probably alludes to the baptism of Jesus Christ when the Holy Spirit came upon him. See also ISA 42:1, ISA 61:1

The seal of the Holy Spirit in the life of believers

The seal marks God's ownership

2CO 1:21–1:22 See also REV 7:3–7:4, REV 9:4, REV 14:1, REV 22:4

The seal acts as a guarantee of redemption

EPH 1:13–1:14 See also 2CO 5:5; EPH 4:30; 1PE 1:5

title: 3290: Holy Spirit, the life-giver
content:
Through the Holy Spirit, God gives birth to and supports both natural and spiritual life. For this reason, Scripture likens the Holy Spirit to life-giving water.

The creating Spirit (or breath) of God gives and sustains life

GEN 1:2 See also GEN 2:7; JOB 26:13, JOB 27:3, JOB 32:8, JOB 33:4, JOB 34:14; PSA 33:6, PSA 104:30

The Holy Spirit gives new life

EZK 37:1–37:14 See also EZK 36:26–36:28

The Holy Spirit is the life-giver in the conception of Jesus Christ

MAT 1:18 See also MAT 1:20; LUK 1:35

The Holy Spirit gives resurrection life to Jesus Christ

1PE 3:18 See also ROM 1:4

The Holy Spirit brings new spiritual life

JHN 3:5 See also JHN 3:6, JHN 3:8, JHN 6:63

The Spirit sets believers free from bondage to works that lead to death

ROM 8:2 See also ROM 8:6, ROM 8:13; GAL 6:8

The Holy Spirit is described as life-giving water

JHN 7:37–7:39 Words spoken by Jesus Christ at the Feast of Tabernacles. See also ISA 32:15, ISA 44:3–44:4; EZK 36:25–36:27, EZK 39:29, EZK 47:1–47:12; ZEC 14:8; JHN 4:10, JHN 4:14; REV 22:1–22:2, REV 22:17

title: 3293: Holy Spirit, witness of
content:
The Holy Spirit bears witness to the person and work of Jesus Christ, and enables believers to bear effective witness to him.

At Jesus Christ's baptism

JHN 1:32–1:34 See also MAT 3:16 parallels MRK 1:10, LUK 3:22

After Jesus Christ's ascension

JHN 15:26 See also JHN 14:26, JHN 16:13–16:14; 1JN 5:6

He brings assurance to believers

HEB 10:14–10:17

He gives believers confidence in their status as children of God

title: 3296: Holy Spirit, work in the world
content:
The Holy Spirit, active in the created world, works to show the futility and sinfulness of life without God, and brings a conviction of the presence of God, his righteousness and coming judgment.

The Holy Spirit is active in the created world

In creation itself

JOB 33:4; PSA 33:6 See also GEN 1:2; JOB 26:13; PSA 104:30

In ordering the natural world

ISA 34:11–34:17

The Holy Spirit is present everywhere in the world

PSA 139:7

The unbelieving world will not recognise the Holy Spirit

JHN 14:17 John uses “the world” to describe those who live apart from God. See also 1CO 2:14

The Holy Spirit testifies to the truth

JHN 15:26 See also ROM 8:16; 1CO 2:11–2:14

The Holy Spirit convicts the world

JHN 16:7–16:11 See also 1CO 14:24–14:25

The Holy Spirit empowers the church to serve the world in mission

ACT 1:8 See also JHN 15:26–15:27
